Valero Energy Expands Credit Agreement With Up to $5.5 Billion Revolving Commitment
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton


Valero Energy amended and restated its existing revolving credit agreement for up to $5.5 billion.

The agreement provides a revolving credit facility of up to $4 billion, with a letter of credit subfacility of up to $2.4 billion. Revolving commitments under the facility may be increased by up to $1.5 billion.

The petroleum refiner entered into the agreement with JPMorgan Chase.

The agreement requires Valero to pay a commitment fee accruing on the daily amount of used and unused commitments at a rate ranging from 0.1% to 0.25% per year.

The interest rate will either be a term SOFR Rate plus a margin of 0.9% to 1.5% a year, or an alternate base rate plus a margin of 0% to 0.5% a year. Valero can choose between the two rates.


Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-16-25 1741ET

Utilities Down, but not by Much, on Defensive Demand - Utilities Roundup
data.description:
Shares of power producers fell, but not by as much as the broad market, as traders sought out defensive sectors.

In West Texas, natural-gas-fired power generation is under construction as part of the $500 billion Stargate data-center project from OpenAI and Oracle, one of several instances where the power-hungry computing facilities will come with their own generation capacity.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-16-25 1740ET

data.title: Communications Services Down Sharply on Flight From Risk - Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
Communications-services companies fell sharply as traders rotated out of high-risk sectors.

The SPDR Select Sector Communications Services exchange-traded fund, which tracks the communication services industry group of the S&P 500, fell by 1%, but remains more than 17% higher for the year to date.

T-Mobile US was more or less flat after analysts at brokerage Wells Fargo boosted their rating on the carrier, saying profit and cash flow growth was likely to accelerate in 2026.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-16-25 1740ET

data.title: Tech Down as Financial Worries Offset AI Optimism - Tech Roundup
data.description:
Shares of technology companies fell as signs of financial stress offset optimism about the artificial-intelligence boom.

Chinese contract manufacturer Foxconn Technology shares surged after its chairman said he met with ChatGPT maker OpenAI and plans to meet with chip giant Nvidia.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing boosted its earnings projection in anticipation of further demand from AI applications.

The U.S. Chamber of Commerce sued the Trump administration over hefty new fees in the H-1B visa program, joining the legal campaign against the administration's changes to a program used by some of the biggest tech companies.

Hewlett Packard Enterprise slid after the provider of servers, storage services and other technology's growth projections for the year lagged lofty Wall Street expectations.

A rout in high-risk cryptocurrencies continued, with bitcoin trading at its lowest level since August. Similarly, shares of quantum-computing firms slid.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-16-25 1740ET

data.title: Financials Down After Zions Warning - Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions fell sharply as a resurgence of credit fears offset strong earnings from the sector.

Regional bank Zions Bancorp dropped by more than one-tenth after disclosing an unexpected loss on two major loans, the latest sign of hidden credit stress as lenders navigate economic uncertainty.

Meanwhile, Western Alliance said it filed a lawsuit in August against one of its borrowers, alleging the company committed fraud.

Other regional lenders, such as Comerica and Fifth Third Bancorp, who recently agreed to merge, fell in sympathy.

Shares of Jefferies Financial, whose losses on loans related to bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands triggered the credit scare, fell by roughly 10%.

JPMorgan Chief Executive Jamie Dimon rattled market confidence when he responded to the bankruptcy of auto lender Tricolor by warning: "when you see one cockroach, there are probably more."

The SPDR Select Sector Financial exchange-traded fund, which tracks the financial industry group of the S&P 500, fell by 2.8%, the biggest loss since the trade-war scare in April.

Shares of custodian bank Bank of New York Mellon slipped after it posted higher third-quarter profit, boosted by a surge in net interest income and fees revenue.

Brokerage Charles Schwab logged earnings growth amid a surge in retail-investment activity.

Federal Reserve officials are divided over the next move even as the government shutdown deprives officials of the data points that could clarify the balance between inflation and economic-slowdown risks.

U.S. Bancorp notched a rise in earnings for the latest quarter thanks to record revenue and loan growth.

Property-and-casualty insurer Travelers slipped after its earnings growth lagged some investors' expectations.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-16-25 1740ET

data.title: Consumer Cos Down on Credit Fears - Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies fell sharply amid fears that signs of credit stress in the banking system reflected a slowdown in the U.S. economy.

Additional supply-chain costs related to tariffs could add $1.2 trillion in 2025 costs for U.S. corporations, warned analysts at research firm S&P Global Market Intelligence, "at least two-thirds of which is being passed on to consumers."

The price increases could slow holiday sales, despite a recent rebound in demand. Luxury firms and deep discounters have reported demand growth in the third quarter.

Ray-Ban glasses maker EssilorLuxottica said it entered the final three months of the year with strong momentum after posting record-breaking quarterly revenue that beat analysts' expectations.

Nestle said it plans to reduce its workforce by 16,000 as new Chief Executive Philipp Navratil seeks to drive cost savings at the world's largest packaged-food company.

Swedish household goods purveyor IKEA is considering rare price increases after new tariffs targeting furniture came into effect this week, require a change of tack.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-16-25 1740ET

data.title: Health Care Cos Edge Lower - Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies fell, but not by as much as the broad market, as traders rotated into sectors less dependent on credit markets and economic growth.

Bavarian Nordic's largest shareholder rejected a sweetened bid from private-equity firms for the Danish vaccine maker, casting doubt over whether the $3.1 billion deal will go through.

Praxis Precision Medicines surged after the biotech firm reported promising late-stage trial of a treatment for a tremor condition.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-16-25 1740ET

data.title: Industrials Down on Credit Worries - Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies fell as signs of credit stress caused a flight from economically cyclical sectors.

"The biggest issue seems to be trade uncertainty with China, along with the lack of economic data due to the government shutdown," said Louis Navellier, a veteran Wall Street strategist. "Economic data still available, such as from the Philly Fed, reflect a modestly softer employment picture, but spending and generally economic activity appear stable."

European defense contractors such as Airbus and Thyssenkrupp have been on a winning streak this year as European nations increase spending on munitions. In the latest development, the European Union will encourage its member states to jointly procure drone and air defense equipment as part of a five-year strategy to rearm and deter Russia.

Logistics firm J.B. Hunt Transport Services rose after it reported third-quarter earnings ahead of Wall Street expectations.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-16-25 1740ET

data.title: Materials Down on Banking Fears - Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials fell amid fears of major credit losses in the banking system.

Gold futures, which investors turn to during financial and geopolitical shocks, rose 2.5% to $4280.20 an ounce, continuing a torrid run.

Cocoa futures climbed rose 3.4% to $6,006 a metric ton after a lower-than-anticipated reading of European volumes of processed cocoa, but remain down 48% year to date.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

Banks in talks with US Treasury to lend $20 billion to Argentina, Semafor reports
data.description:
(Reuters) -Banks including JPMorgan, Bank of America, Goldman Sachs and Citigroup are in talks with the U.S. Treasury to provide up to $20 billion in loans to Argentina, Semafor reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Wednesday had said that the department was working with banks and investment funds to create a $20 billion facility to invest in the South American country's sovereign debt.

Bessent said that the facility would sit alongside a new $20 billion U.S. currency swap line for Argentina, providing a total of $40 billion in support for Latin America's third-largest economy.

The banks are discussing an emergency loan backed by Argentine assets, Semafor said, adding that talks are ongoing and specific collateral is still being ironed out.

BofA, Goldman Sachs and Citigroup declined to comment, while JPMorgan did not immediately respond when contacted by Reuters.

President Donald Trump said on Tuesday that the U.S. would not "waste our time" with Argentina if President Javier Milei's party loses in parliamentary elections on October 26.

But Bessent clarified that the U.S. would continue to support Argentina financially as long as Milei's government pursues "good policies," regardless of the election outcome.

(Reporting by Juby Babu in Mexico City; Editing by Alan Barona)

Another Apple exec leaves for Meta as AI talent war heats up, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
(Corrects October 15 story in paragraph 6 to indicate that Bloomberg did not report Robby Walker left Apple for Meta Platforms)

(Reuters) -Apple's Ke Yang, the newly tapped executive leading an effort to develop a ChatGPT-like AI-driven web search, is stepping down to join Meta, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Just weeks ago, Yang was appointed head of a team called Answers, Knowledge and Information, or AKI, which is central to the Siri voice assistant's overhaul, planned for March, the report said.

Apple, Meta and Yang did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

Yang has been with Apple since 2019, according to his LinkedIn profile.  

Meta has intensified Silicon Valley's talent war through aggressive hiring aiming to challenge rivals, including OpenAI, Google and Anthropic, as the tech firms pour significant investment into AI in the race to superintelligence.

The Mark Zuckerberg-led company had poached top AI executives from the iPhone maker before, including Ruoming Pang, as earlier reported by Bloomberg News. Bloomberg News also reported that Robby Walker left Apple, without specifying a new employer.

(Reporting by Ruchika Khanna in Bengaluru; Editing by Alan Barona)

data.title: Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria, S.A. (BME : BBVA) cancelled the acquisition of Banco de Sabadell, S.A. (BME : SAB)
data.description:
Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria, S.A. (BME : BBVA) made an offer to acquire Banco de Sabadell, S.A. (BME : SAB) for ?11.5 billion on April 30, 2024. As consideration, 1 newly issued BBVA share will be issued for every 4.83 Banco Sabadell shares. After the merger, Banco Sabadell shareholders would have a 16% stake in the resulting entity. Post completion of the acquisition, the management team of the resulting entity would be made up of executives from both banks. The merged entity would have one of its Group?s operational headquarters in Catalonia, which would be established at Banco Sabadell's corporate center in Sant Cugat. The corporate name and brand would be BBVA, the use of Banco Sabadell brand would be maintained, together with the BBVA brand, in those regions or businesses where it may have relevant commercial interest. As of September 22, 2025, the transaction terms has been revised, Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria, S.A. will acquire Banco de Sabadell, S.A for ?17.9 billion. As per the new terms the consideration will be entirely exchange consideration of newly issued ordinary shares of BBVA, at an exchange ratio of one ordinary BBVA share for every 4.8376 ordinary shares of Banco Sabadell.

The merger would be subject to obtaining the relevant authorizations or declarations of non-opposition from the competent supervisors and from the competition authorities with jurisdiction. The terms in this proposal have been approved by BBVA's Board of Directors. As of May 6, 2024, BBVA cancelled the acquisition of Banco de Sabadell, S.A. as the Board believes that the Proposal significantly undervalues the potential of Banco Sabadell and its standalone growth prospects. The decision to re-launch the Offer was adopted by a resolution approved by the Offeror?s Board of Directors at its meeting held on May 8, 2024. As of May 9, 2024, the terminated transaction re-opened with same terms offered by BBVA's, an exchange of one newly issued BBVA share for every 4.83 of Banco Sabadell. The new proposal is subject to acquiring more than 50.01 percent of Banco Sabadell, the approval of the Shareholders? General Meeting, and the approvals of the Spanish Market and Competition regulator and the U.K.?s Prudential Regulation Authority, the most important are, of course, ECB and the CNMV and the CNMC, the antitrust authorities in Spain. The closing of the operation is expected to take between six to eight months. As of May 9, 2024, the Board of Directors of BBVA has approved the offer. The transaction is accretive in earnings per share (EPS) from the first year after the merger. As of May 24, 2024, BBVA bank formalized its hostile bid for Banc Sabadell by submitting the request for approval to the Spanish stock market regulator (CNMV). On May 6, 2024, The board cancelled the deal because the board believes that the Proposal significantly undervalues the potential of Banco Sabadell and its standalone growth prospects. The Board of Banco de Sabadell has rejected BBVA?s Proposal. As of September 5, 2024, European Central Bank approved the transaction. As on October 1, 2024, the offer has been revised to 1 newly issued ordinary share of BBVA and ?0.29 in cash for 5.0196 ordinary shares of Banco Sabadell. The effectiveness of the Offer is contingent upon obtaining the authorization from the CNMC. As of November 26, 2024, European Commission has approved the deal. As of December 23, 2024, The Mexican Federal Economic Competition Commission approved the deal. As of January 31, 2024, The Basque Competition Authority approved the transaction. As of April 30, 2025, The National Commission for Markets and Competition has approved BBVA's hostile takeover bid for Banco Sabadell. As of September 12, 2025. Banco Sabadell?s Board of Directors has unanimously recommended that its shareholders reject BBVA?s share exchange offer. The exchange offer will expire on currently October 10, 2025, but this date may be extended by the CNMV in accordance with the Spanish Takeover Regulation. As of September 30, 2025, Banco de Sabadell's board rejected the enhanced offer as its represents lower than the value of Banco Sabadell, and, consequently considers that it is in the best interests of Banco Sabadell?s shareholders not to accept the Offer. As of October 8, 2025, The CNMV is expected to announce the bid?s result on October 17.

In a filing with Spain?s securities regulator CNMV on Tuesday, Sabadell?s board said the offer fails to reflect the bank?s true value

J.P. Morgan SE, UBS Europe SE and Rothschild & Co SCA acted as financial advisors and J&A Garrigues, S.L.P. and Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP acted as legal advisors to BBVA. Evercore, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley acted as financial advisers and Uría Menéndez Abogados, S.L.P. acted as legal advisors to Banco de Sabadell, S.A.

Banco Bilbao Vizcaya Argentaria, S.A. (BME : BBVA) cancelled the acquisition of Banco de Sabadell, S.A. (BME : SAB) on October 16, 2025. The National Securities Market Commission (CNMV) has published the results of BBVA's takeover bid for Banco Sabadell. The offer was accepted by Banco Sabadell shareholders holding 25.5% of the voting rights, rendering the offer void as the minimum acceptance condition was not met.

data.title: Bank of America Corporation Announces Redemption of $2,500,000,000 1.197% Fixed/Floating Rate Senior Notes, Due October 2026
data.description:
Bank of America Corporation announced that it will redeem on October 24, 2025 all $2,500,000,000 principal amount outstanding of its 1.197% Fixed/Floating Rate Senior Notes, due October 2026 (CUSIP No. 06051GJK6) (the "Notes"), at a redemption price equal to 100% of the principal amount of the Notes, plus accrued and unpaid interest to, but excluding, the redemption date of October 24, 2025. Interest on the Notes will cease to accrue on the redemption date.

Payment of the redemption price for the Notes will be made through the facilities of The Depository Trust Company. The Bank of New York Mellon Trust Company, N.A. is the trustee and paying agent for the Notes.

data.title: Equifax Inc. Introduces Equifax Ignite®? AI Advisor
data.description:
Equifax Inc. announced the launch of Equifax Ignite®? AI Advisor, a new solution designed to help lenders pinpoint new opportunities, grow accounts, and make confident informed decisions at speed on one platform. Powered by a secure Agentic-AI enabled data and analytics engine on the Equifax Cloud??, Equifax Ignite AI Advisor is built with Equifax Amplify AI??.

Part of a growing suite of Equifax AI-enabled solutions and new to the Equifax Ignite ecosystem, EquifaxIgnite AI Advisor has been purpose-built to empower busy lenders to quickly build comprehensive, actionable views of what matters most to their business by forgoing the everyday complexities associated with data and analytics. Equifax Ignite AI Advisor uses a lender's own data, alongside data provided by Equifax, to create clear, actionable insights that drive more-informed decision-making. These insights are secure and not shared outside of the lender's own view.

Through a no- setup consultative experience, the solution delivers support for smaller and mid-sized financial institutions that may have limited access to dedicated data-scientists. Using natural language, Equifax Ignite AI advisor recommends actions a lender can take to resolve a potential issue, drive growth, or otherwise optimize lending workflows and outcomes. Equifax Ignite AI advisor enables lenders to: Benchmark Against the Competition - Compare key metrics like delinquency against peers, origination risk changes over time, and market share relative to peer performance.

Find Growth Opportunities - Get practical recommendations for actions to take based on intelligence into wallet share and performance against peers. Make Confident Decisions - Ask questions through a generative AI chat with complementary visual dashboardrations, dynamic charts and graphs, to easily compare information, discover new trends and create new offers for consumers. Level the Playing Field - Obtain curated AI-driven insights that can benefit small and mid-sized financial institutions who may have limited access to in-house data scientists.

Bundestag Extends Law Protecting Parcel Delivery Workers
data.description:
BERLIN (dpa-AFX) - The Bundestag has extended the validity of a law designed to provide social protection for parcel delivery workers. The regulation, which would have expired at the end of the year without parliamentary approval, introduces what is known as subcontractor liability. This means that shipping companies are held accountable if their subcontractors fail to pay social security contributions.

The law first came into effect in 2019, as many parcel couriers are employed by such subcontractors -- but it was initially set to expire at the end of 2025. However, according to SPD lawmaker Jan Dieren, the regulation has proven effective: "Since then, large companies have been sending their own staff much more frequently." As a result, the number of jobs in the parcel sector subject to social security contributions has increased significantly.

Marketers Gain Enhanced Precision Through Proxima's Collaboration with Microsoft Curate for Commerce
data.description:
Proxima announced that they are leveraging Microsoft Curate for Commerce to enable advertisers to target their social-first, first-party ecommerce transaction-based audiences across Microsoft Monetize supply. These audience deals will be offered exclusively via Microsoft Advertising and can be activated via advertisers' DSP of choice. With activation on Microsoft Curate for Commerce, marketers gain access to: Privacy-forward audience segments informed by deterministic commerce signals; Improved efficiency through superior targeting, creative delivery, and optimized placement; Incremental campaign performance powered by transactional data from real shopping outcomes.

Because marketers can activate in their DSP of choice, they can integrate this buy into their existing workflows, driving operational efficiency. Proxima is available for pilot activations with select advertisers and agencies. Brands and partners can contact Proxima to scope category coverage, onboarding pathways, and measurement plans.

data.title: Niron Magnetics and Stellantis Partner to Pioneer Rare-Earth-Free Electric Motor Innovation
data.description:
Niron Magnetics announced a project in collaboration with Stellantis to develop a new generation of electric motor designs for automotive use using magnets free of rare-earth elements. Niron's breakthrough Iron Nitride magnet technology offers a promising alternative to traditional rare-earth-based magnets, helping to reduce dependence on foreign-dominated supply chains. The collaboration builds upon Stellantis Ventures' strategic investment in Niron Magnetics in 2023.

It features additional support from the U.S. Department of Energy's Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO), which contributed $2.7 million to the project as part of its strategy to strengthen American energy independence and secure critical materials. Permanent magnets play a vital role in dozens of vehicle systems, from audio and power steering to fluid pumps and powertrains. Magnets are critical components in a wide range of powertrains, including those used in internal combustion engines, hybrids, and electric vehicles.

Traditional permanent magnets rely heavily on rare earth elements, primarily sourced from China. In contrast, Niron Magnetics' Iron Nitride magnets are made from abundant iron and atmospheric nitrogen, materials readily available in the United States and globally. The Stellantis-Niron project aims to enhance motor architecture across all vehicle types by leveraging the unique properties of Iron Nitride magnets to deliver improved performance and efficiency.

The Stellantis- Niron project aims to enhance motor Architecture across all vehicle types by leveraging The unique properties of Iron Nitride magnet to deliver improved performance and efficiency". Titled " variable Flux Field Intensifying Motors Using Iron Nitride Permanent Magnets," the joint project builds on Stellantis' investment and ongoing collaboration with Niron Magnetics in areas such as traction motors, automotive audio, and other magnet-driven systems. The announcement coincides with the groundbreaking of Niron Magnetics' first commercial-scale manufacturing facility in Sartell, Minnesota. When fully operational, the site will be one of North America's largest magnet production facilities, producing 1,500 tons of magnets annually and creating 175 full-time jobs.

Niron Magnetics and Stellantis will also collaborate with leading research institutions, including Oak Ridge National Laboratory and the Illinois Institute of Technology, to leverage their expertise in advanced motor systems analysis and testing.

data.title: Artisight Introduces Smart Hospital Rooms with Ambient Documentation and Touchless Authentication
data.description:
Artisight announced a pilot to transform clinical workflows by extending Microsoft Dragon Copilot with the Artisight Smart Hospital Platform. Connecting Artisight and Dragon Copilot will bring ambient AI technology into the hospital room itself, creating a hands-free and device-free documentation interface for nurses. At the core of this capability is Artisight's ability to pair each nurse's unique voice profile with their real-time location system (RTLS) badge for two-factor authentication for security, eliminating the need for a device, manual logins or physical contact with shared workstations.

With this collaboration, documentation can happen in the background while nurses walk into the room and start caring for their patients. The pilot is currently underway at a leading hospital innovation center, with plans for a finalized extension with Microsoft Dragon Copilot following comprehensive clinical validation. During the initial pilot, nurses said they were able to document with higher frequency, and the AI-generated clinical summaries delivered a better, more accurate reflection of the care provided during the encounter.

Nurses also said the hands-free platform would better enable them to provide hands-on patient care and save time on documentation. Hospitals can expect measurable improvements in efficiency, accuracy, and compliance. This development builds on Artisight's mission to unify hospital operations through AI-powered computer vision, ambient sensors, and real-time communication.

Smart hospital rooms equipped with these capabilities showcase the future of healthcare delivery--where technology fades into the background and clinicians can dedicate their attention to what matters most: caring for patients.
